touches.ai_summary: Inbound request from Grace Wilson, Director of CS at CentralSpark, expressing interest in our platform for improving rep productivity. Brief form submission indicating they're currently evaluating alternatives including Chorus.

Initial qualification call with Grace. Discussed CentralSpark's current challenges with rep onboarding and coaching consistency across their CS team. Grace mentioned they're a 87-person fintech with growing customer success needs. Confirmed budget authority and timeline.

questions_asked.question: What's driving your search for a conversation intelligence platform right now?
questions_asked.answer: We've doubled our CS team in 6 months and need better coaching tools to maintain quality

questions_asked.question: How does your pricing compare to Chorus? We're also evaluating them.
questions_asked.answer: Our unlimited automation tier at your team size typically comes in 20-30% lower, and I can walk through the feature differences

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up qualification call diving deeper into technical requirements and team structure. Grace walked through their current tech stack and integration needs. Discussed use cases around rep productivity and coaching workflows. Confirmed 4 initial seats for CS leadership.

questions_asked.question: Can you integrate with our existing CRM and support ticketing system?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we have native integrations with all major CRMs and can set up custom webhooks if needed

questions_asked.question: Who else should be involved in evaluating this beyond yourself?
questions_asked.answer: Sophia Klein, one of our Account Managers, will be a key user. Our VP of Ops will need to sign off eventually

next_steps: Prepare demo focused on coaching workflows

touches.ai_summary: Brief discovery call to finalize demo agenda. Grace confirmed key pain points around inconsistent coaching and limited visibility into rep performance. Noted they want to see automation capabilities that differentiate from Chorus. Short call due to Grace's scheduling conflict.

questions_asked.question: What would a successful first 90 days look like with our platform?
questions_asked.answer: All CS managers using it for weekly coaching sessions and having automated call summaries flowing into our CRM

touches.ai_summary: Pre-demo email to Grace confirming agenda and sharing relevant case study from similar fintech customer. Requested confirmation that Sophia would be joining the demo session.

questions_asked.question: Can you send the integration documentation beforehand so our team can review?
questions_asked.answer: Absolutely, I've attached our integration guide and API docs

touches.ai_summary: Demo session with Grace and Sophia. Walked through core coaching features, automation workflows, and CRM integration. Sophia asked detailed questions about end-user experience. Grace seemed engaged but mentioned they're still in active conversations with Chorus. No executive presence on the call.

questions_asked.question: How customizable are the automated scorecards for our specific use cases?
questions_asked.answer: Fully customizable - you can create custom rubrics and criteria sets for different call types

questions_asked.question: What's the typical implementation timeline?
questions_asked.answer: Most customers are fully onboarded within 2-3 weeks including integrations and team training

objections: Chorus offers some features in their base tier that seem to require your automation tier
next_steps: Send proposal with unlimited automation tier pricing

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email sending formal proposal for 4 seats at $3,900 ACV with unlimited automation tier. Addressed the Chorus feature comparison with detailed breakdown. Asked about timeline for decision and next steps with executive stakeholders.
next_steps: Grace to review internally and get back by end of week

touches.ai_summary: Check-in email after proposal. Grace responded that pricing looks reasonable but she needs to loop in VP of Ops before moving forward. Asked if there's any flexibility on pricing given they're comparing with Chorus. Mentioned timeline is tightening up.

questions_asked.question: Is there any room for negotiation if we commit to annual payment upfront?
questions_asked.answer: Let me discuss with my manager - I may be able to offer a small discount for annual prepay

next_steps: Schedule call with VP of Ops

touches.ai_summary: Extended executive meeting with Grace, but VP of Ops had a last-minute conflict and didn't join. Grace drove the conversation, going deep on contract terms, implementation support, and comparing our approach to Chorus. Discussion around pricing flexibility and annual vs quarterly billing. Grace seems committed but unclear on final decision authority without executive sign-off.

questions_asked.question: Can you include additional implementation support in the package if we move forward this quarter?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we can bundle in extra onboarding hours and a dedicated CSM for the first 60 days

questions_asked.question: When will you be able to get time with your VP of Ops to finalize?
questions_asked.answer: He's been swamped but should have bandwidth next week. I can champion this internally in the meantime

questions_asked.question: What's your best and final pricing for annual prepay?
questions_asked.answer: I can offer $3,600 ACV for annual prepayment, which is about 8% off

objections: We need to make sure this integrates smoothly with our existing workflow - Chorus has been very responsive on the technical side
next_steps: Grace to schedule VP of Ops meeting
Send revised proposal with annual prepay pricing
